summary: British-American landscape painter, founder of the Hudson River School. Most commonly identified by The Oxbow, The Course of Empire (5-painting series), and The Voyage of Life (4-painting series). Patron: Luman Reed. Depicted alongside William Cullen Bryant in Asher Durand's Kindred Spirits. Also painted The Titan's Goblet and The Architect's Dream.

works.name: The Course of Empire (1833–1836)
works.description: Series of five paintings depicting the rise and fall of a civilization. Commissioned by Luman Reed. Inspired by a line from Byron's Childe Harold's Pilgrimage. Each painting depicts the same rocky outcrop with a precariously balanced boulder. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Five-painting series depicting the rise and fall of a civilization/city
examples: a series depicting the growth and destruction of a city
a series of five paintings by this artist bookended by The Savage State and Desolation
this 5-painting series depicting the rise and fall of a classical civilization

clues.clue: Begins with The Savage State (hunter, canoes, wigwams, man chasing deer)
examples: begins with a bare landscape titled The Savage State
this painting, which depicts a hunter, canoes, and several wigwams
the first shows a man chasing a deer through foliage

clues.clue: Ends with Desolation (ruined city overrun by nature, mossy/ivy-covered column, broken marble columns)
examples: a mossy pillar in the left of one piece, part of a series depicting the rise and fall of an imaginary city
A pair of birds nest atop an ivy-covered column in this painting's foreground...a ruined city overrun by nature

clues.clue: Destruction (4th): headless statue; woman throwing herself into harbour; based on Borghese Gladiator sculpture
examples: Destruction, includes a headless statue on the right-hand side
a woman throwing herself into a harbour to avoid the sexual advances from a soldier
Destruction prominently features a sculpture based on [the Borghese Gladiator]

clues.clue: Includes The Arcadian or Pastoral State (2nd) and The Consummation of Empire (3rd)
examples: a painting subtitled The Arcadian or Pastoral State
this painter who also painted The Consummation of Empire

clues.clue: Same rocky outcrop with a precariously balanced boulder appears in each painting
examples: each picture depicts the same rocky outcrop with a precariously balanced boulder

clues.clue: Commissioned by Luman Reed
examples: Luman Reed commissioned a series by this artist

clues.clue: Inspired by Byron's Childe Harold's Pilgrimage ("wealth, vice, corruption")
examples: inspired by a line from Childe Harold's Pilgrimage about "wealth, vice, [and] corruption"
Inspired by Childe Harolde's Pilgrimage

clues.clue: Ed Ruscha made a 10-painting cycle inspired by Cole's, showing Los Angeles building tops
examples: A different 10-painting cycle titled The Course of Empire, inspired by Cole's, shows changes in the building tops of this city

works.name: The Oxbow (1836)
works.description: View from Mount Holyoke after a thunderstorm, depicting a bend in the Connecticut River. Cole includes a tiny self-portrait of himself painting in the foreground. Wikipedia

clues.clue: View from Mount Holyoke of a bend in the Connecticut River, after a thunderstorm
examples: encouraged that artist to go to Mount Holyoke and paint a river bend in the aftermath of a thunderstorm
He painted the aftermath of a thunderstorm rolling over an oxbow river in Massachusetts.
a gnarled, leaning tree in the foreground as the Connecticut River flows in the distance

clues.clue: Cole includes a very small self-portrait of himself painting in the foreground/forest
examples: This artist made a very small depiction of himself painting in a forest that overlooks a bend in the Connecticut River.
also included a small self-portrait of himself in the foreground of his painting The Oxbow

clues.clue: Commissioned by Luman Reed
examples: This artist's frequent patron Luman Reed commissioned a work by them set "after a Thunderstorm" viewed from Mount Holyoke.

clues.clue: Some claim upside-down Hebrew letters reading "Shaddai" on a mountain in the background
examples: Some claim that an artist with this first name painted upside-down Hebrew letters reading "Shaddai" on a mountain in the background of one work.

clues.clue: Tyler Green 2021 book examines concurrent creation with an Emerson essay describing a similar landscape
examples: A 2021 Tyler Green book examines the concurrent creation of a painting by this artist and an essay that describes a similar landscape made up of "twenty or thirty farms."

works.name: The Voyage of Life (1842)
works.description: Series of four allegorical paintings depicting a man's journey down a river in a golden boat, guided by an angel. Stages: Childhood, Youth, Manhood, Old Age. Second set at the National Gallery of Art. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Four allegorical paintings: man in a boat journeying down a river through stages of life (Childhood, Youth, Manhood, Old Age)
examples: this four-part allegorical painting series which depicts a journey down a river
a man in a boat as the landscape around him changes
a boy's life journey down a river in a golden boat

clues.clue: An angel accompanies the man throughout (guardian angel in white)
examples: a man's guardian angel leads him on eternal boat ride as he grows older
One of these beings can barely be seen in the upper left of the painting Manhood, but is prevalent in the other three
A man in red and an angel in white use one of these objects

clues.clue: Youth (2nd): boy/man points to a heavenly white castle in the sky; hourglass on the boat
examples: a man points to a white castle in the sky as an angel stands to his right
a boy raises his hand towards a heavenly castle while standing in a boat that has an hourglass attached to it

clues.clue: Old Age (4th): old man praying, angel appears, gestures toward light; impending peace of death
examples: an old man praying in a boat who is made joyous by the appearance of an angel, who gestures towards a light
The last painting in this series, Old Age, shows a bearded man near death.

clues.clue: Childhood (1st): angel carved into the front of the boat
examples: One of these figures is carved into the front of a boat in the first painting in a series.

clues.clue: First three paintings depict an hourglass attached to the boat
examples: The first three paintings in this series depict an hourglass attached to a boat

works.name: The Titan's Goblet (1833)
works.description: Fantastical painting of a gigantic drinking vessel / goblet standing on a hill, with boats sailing on water inside it and a Greek temple on the brim. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Huge drinking vessel / goblet / cup with boats sailing on water inside it
examples: a huge drinking vessel with boats sailing on the surface
boats sail in a massive earthen cup
several ships sail in an ocean contained in the oversized title object

clues.clue: Two tiny buildings / Greek temple and vegetation on the brim
examples: Two tiny buildings and a temple can be seen on the brim of the title object
Vegetation grows on the title object's brim, which contains a Greek temple

clues.clue: Stands on a hill, dwarfing the tiny human village below
examples: The title structure of this painting stands on a hill dwarfing the tiny human village below.

clues.clue: May have been influenced by Turner's painting of Ulysses and Polyphemus (per art historian Ellwood Parry)
examples: Cole's painting of The Titan's Goblet may have been influenced by his viewing of a J.M.W. Turner painting depicting these two mythological characters

works.name: The Architect's Dream (1840)
works.description: Commissioned by architect Ithiel Town (who rejected it). A figure reclines on a column atop books, overlooking a fantastical landscape of Greek and Gothic architecture. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Commissioned by architect Ithiel Town (who rejected it); framed by a green curtain
examples: Ithiel Town commissioned a painting by this artist that is framed by a green curtain
rejected by a man with its title profession, Ithiel Town
a figure reclining on a pile of out-of-proportion books on top of a stone slab referencing that work's commissioner, Ithiel Town

clues.clue: Figure reclines on a pile of books on top of a large column/pillar; pyramid and enormous Greek temple in background
examples: A pyramid appears in that painting's background, whose title subject lies on a stack of books on top of a large column.
a man laying on a pillar in front of a pyramid and an enormous Greek temple

clues.clue: Gothic cathedral alongside Greek columns
examples: This artist showed a Gothic cathedral alongside Greek columns in The Architect's Dream

works.name: Kindred Spirits (by Asher Durand, 1849)
works.description: Painted by Asher Durand, not Cole — but Cole is depicted in it. Shows Cole and poet William Cullen Bryant standing on a ledge at Kaaterskill Falls in the Catskills. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Cole depicted with William Cullen Bryant on a ledge/rock in the Catskill Mountains by Asher Durand
examples: This artist is depicted conversing with William Cullen Bryant in his contemporary Asher Durand's painting Kindred Spirits.
Cole and the poet William Cullen Bryant standing on a promontory in the Catskill Mountains
This artist stands on an outcrop with William Cullen Bryant in Asher Durand's painting Kindred Spirits.

clues.clue: Cole wears a straw hat and holds a portfolio; Bryant leans on a cane; Cole points with a paintbrush at Kaaterskill Falls
examples: this man is depicted wearing a straw hat and holding a portfolio while talking to William Cullen Bryant
a companion points with a paintbrush at the Kaaterskill Falls

clues.clue: Commissioned by Jonathan Sturges after Cole's death; purchased by Alice Walton in 2005
examples: Jonathan Sturges commissioned a painting by this artist depicting a recently deceased man
In 2005, Alice Walton purchased this Asher Durand painting

clues.clue: Landscape with Figures and Cora Kneeling at the Feet of Tamenund — paintings based on The Last of the Mohicans (4-painting series)
examples: Landscape with Figures is one of four paintings based on a novel that this artist exhibited at the National Gallery of Design
Cora Kneeling at the Feet of Tamenund depicts a scene from The Last of the Mohicans
Cole also painted a series of four works that depict scenes from this novel

clues.clue: Il Penseroso — woman falls at a shrine of Saint Mary
examples: A woman falls at a shrine of Saint Mary in this artist's painting of the poem Il Penseroso.

clues.clue: The Past and The Present — pair of paintings centered on a castle becoming ruined over time
examples: A pair of Thomas Cole paintings, The Past and The Present, center on one of these buildings that has become ruined over time.

clues.clue: The Arch of Nero — painting of Rome (loaned to Philadelphia Museum of Art in 2021 after Newark deaccessioned it)
examples: the Philadelphia Museum of Art was loaned a Thomas Cole painting of this city that the Newark Museum had deaccessioned

works.description: How Cole is most commonly identified.

clues.clue: Founder of the Hudson River School
examples: this Hudson River School painter of The Oxbow and The Course of Empire

this American artist who painted The Architect's Dream and The Oxbow in addition to founding the Hudson River School

examples: this American artist of The Course of Empire
this British-American artist

clues.clue: Luman Reed was his frequent patron
examples: This artist's frequent patron Luman Reed

clues.clue: Other Hudson River School members: Asher Durand, Frederic Church

clues.clue: National Historic Site; 2016 exhibition of recreated studio curated by Annette Blaugrund
